title: Piptatherum frutescens
taxonomy: Piptatherum frutescens is classified under the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the tribe Cynodonteae. The genus Piptatherum is part of the subfamily Chloridoideae. This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is also found in parts of Africa and Asia. The exact classification may vary slightly depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Poaceae family.

intro: Piptatherum frutescens, also known as sweet-scented grass or sweet grass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia, valued for its medicinal properties and aromatic scent. The health benefits of Piptatherum frutescens include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving respiratory health.
